hints.junior: Paste just the error, the relevant 20 lines of code, and what you expected. That's enough. The model doesn't need your whole file.
hints.mid: Context window consumption is asymmetric: a full 500-line file uses 10× the budget but adds maybe 20% more signal than the relevant 50 lines. Curate before you paste.
hints.staff: In agentic pipelines, context budget is a hard constraint (128k, 200k tokens). Reserve headroom for chain-of-thought, tool outputs, and multi-turn history. Overfilling kills retrieval quality.

hints.junior: The 'Overloaded' demo shows a model struggling to locate the relevant error among noise. The 'Curated' demo shows the same model answering correctly with the same question but less context.
hints.mid: Watch the model's answer quality drop as irrelevant files enter the context. This mirrors production: RAG pipelines with low-relevance chunks produce worse answers than high-relevance short ones.
hints.staff: Token budget isn't just cost — it's quality. Beyond 50% context fill, many models show degraded recall on content from the middle of the window ('lost in the middle' phenomenon, studied in 2023).

anecdotes.junior: I once pasted an entire 1,200-line component to fix a 3-line bug. The model suggested a refactor instead — it had too much to work with. Next try: paste the function plus the error. Fixed in one shot.
anecdotes.mid: A team I consulted used an AI assistant that auto-included the full repo in every request. After capping each request to 5 most-relevant files via embeddings search, fix accuracy went from 60% to 85%.
anecdotes.staff: The 'lost in the middle' paper (Liu et al., 2023) showed GPT-4 recall dropped to near-random for facts placed in the middle of long contexts. Design your context-assembly pipeline to put critical facts at the start or end.

sections.junior: Hey! Today we're learning how to get better answers from AI coding assistants — by sending less, not more.
sections.mid: This case covers context window budget management: what to include, what to exclude, and how curated context beats document dumps for code-quality tasks.
sections.staff: We're covering the token budget math, the lost-in-the-middle recall degradation, and context-assembly strategies for agentic pipelines — RAG, re-ranking, and headroom budgeting.
